name: HTS 'Deterrence of Aggression' Offensive Launch
detail: HTS and allied factions launched a major offensive from Idlib province on November 27, 2024. Within 72 hours, rebel forces captured western Aleppo; the entire city fell by November 30. The rapid advance exploited Assad-regime military exhaustion and reduced Russian/Iranian support due to Ukraine and Gaza wars.

name: Rebel Forces Capture Hama
detail: HTS and allied rebel factions entered and captured Hama — Syria's fourth largest city — on December 5, 2024, meeting minimal resistance. Government forces withdrew without significant fighting. Hama had been the site of Assad's father's 1982 massacre; its fall without a battle symbolized the profound collapse of regime military will.

name: Rebel Forces Capture Homs — Road to Damascus Opens
detail: Rebel forces captured Homs on December 7, 2024, completing a sweep down Syria's strategic M5 highway. Homs — dubbed 'capital of the revolution' for its early 2011–2012 uprising role — fell to the same forces that toppled Assad's rule, completing a remarkable reversal from the 2016 government retaking. With Homs fell, the road to Damascus was open.

name: Damascus Captured; Assad Flees Syria
detail: Rebel forces entered Damascus on December 8, 2024. Government forces dissolved or changed sides throughout the night. Assad fled on a private jet to Russia. Prison gates were opened, releasing thousands from notorious facilities including Saydnaya. Jubilant crowds gathered in Umayyad Square. The 54-year Assad dynasty ended without a final battle.

name: SDF Drives ISIS from Deir ez-Zor Province
detail: US-backed SDF forces crossed the Euphrates River in September 2017 and drove ISIS from the northern Deir ez-Zor province through November 2017. This brought SDF into direct contact and occasional conflict with Assad forces crossing from the west. The SDF took control of most of Syria's remaining oil fields — a major source of ongoing revenue and political tension.

name: Iran Launches 300+ Projectiles at Israel
detail: In retaliation for Israel's April 1, 2024 strike on the Iranian consulate in Damascus, Iran launched approximately 120 ballistic missiles, 170 drones, and 30 cruise missiles at Israel on April 13–14, 2024. Over 99% were intercepted by a US-Israeli-Jordanian-British air defense coalition. One Israeli child was critically injured. Iran called the attack completed and announced no further retaliation, then scaled back.
